curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 10 units, 14 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Express the concentration of solutions using various units
Describe vapour pressure of liquid solutions
Distinguish between ideal and non-ideal solutions
Apply colligative properties to determine molar mass
Explain the working of electrochemical and galvanic cells
Apply the Nernst Equation in cell potential calculations
Describe processes in electrolytic cells and electrolysis
Differentiate between batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion mechanisms
Define the rate of a chemical reaction

Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid

Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
practicals.details: Preparation of Inorganic Compounds. Mandatory for Section B.

practicals.title: Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, or Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
practicals.details: Preparation of Organic Compounds. Choose any one.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
practicals.details: Food analysis. Mandatory for Section B.

practicals.details: Quantitative analysis. Students prepare standard solutions themselves.

practicals.title: Qualitative analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.formative_topics_note: Surface Chemistry, General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements, Polymers, Chemistry in Everyday life are assessed only formatively.
internal_assessment.choice_policy: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections. No overall choice.

mandatory_experiments_section_b: Quantitative analysis (Oxalic acid standard solution and KMnO4 titration)
Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation)

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

practicals.title: Preparation of one lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and one lyophobic sol
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry. Lyophilic: starch, egg albumin, gum. Lyophobic: aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide.

practicals.title: Dialysis of sol prepared in previous experiment
Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
Study of reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide or Potassium Iodate with Sodium Sulphate (clock reaction)
practicals.details: Chemical Kinetics. Use starch solution as indicator.

practicals.title: Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate
Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid (HCl) and strong base (NaOH)
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn || Cu/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
practicals.details: Chromatography. Distance values may be provided.

practicals.title: Separation of constituents present in an inorganic mixture containing two cations only by paper chromatography
Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
practicals.details: Preparation of Inorganic Compounds. Mandatory for Section B.

practicals.title: Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, or Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
practicals.details: Preparation of Organic Compounds. Choose any one.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
practicals.details: Food analysis. Mandatory for Section B.

practicals.title: Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating against standard Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
practicals.details: Quantitative analysis. Students prepare standard solutions themselves.

practicals.title: Qualitative analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Inorganic Analysis. Cations include Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4+. Anions include CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO.

projects.details: Investigatory project involving laboratory testing.

projects.details: Investigatory project involving laboratory testing and collecting information.

competency_groups.title: Solutions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to types, concentration expressions, solubility, vapor pressure, ideal/non-ideal solutions, and colligative properties.
competencies: Identify different types of solutions
Express the concentration of solutions using various units
Explain factors affecting solubility
Describe vapour pressure of liquid solutions
Distinguish between ideal and non-ideal solutions
Apply colligative properties to determine molar mass

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to electrochemical cells, Nernst equation, conductance, electrolysis, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion.
competencies: Explain the working of electrochemical and galvanic cells
Apply the Nernst Equation in cell potential calculations
Analyze conductance of electrolytic solutions
Describe processes in electrolytic cells and electrolysis
Differentiate between batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion mechanisms

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reaction rates, factors influencing rate, integrated rate equations, temperature dependence, and collision theory.
competencies: Define the rate of a chemical reaction
Analyze factors influencing the rate of reaction
Derive and apply integrated rate equations for zero and first-order reactions
Explain the temperature dependence of reaction rates
Apply collision theory to explain chemical reactivity

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic table position, electronic configuration, properties of transition elements, lanthanoids, actinoids, and applications.
competencies: Locate d-block and f-block elements in the periodic table
Write electronic configurations for d-Block Elements
Describe general properties of Transition Elements (d-Block)
Identify important compounds of transition elements
Differentiate between Lanthanoids and Actinoids
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Werner's theory, nomenclature, isomerism, bonding theories (including metal carbonyls), and applications.
competencies: Explain Werner's Theory of Coordination Compound
Define important terms pertaining to coordination compounds
Apply IUPAC rules for nomenclature of coordination compounds
Identify types of isomerism in coordination compounds
Describe bonding theories including Metal Carbonyls

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
